title: Docking station not detecting monitors after Windows update

servicenow_seed.short_description: [TRAINING] Docking station not detecting external monitors
servicenow_seed.description: User returned to the office after a Windows update overnight. The dock no longer drives either external monitor; laptop screen works.

correct_steps: Verify caller and capture asset tag of laptop and dock
Confirm monitors work directly attached to the laptop (rules out monitors)
Cable swap (DisplayPort/HDMI/USB-C) and dock power cycle (unplug 30s)
Check dock firmware and graphics driver versions; install vendor updates if behind
If symptom persists or affects multiple users on same dock model, escalate
rationale: Dock failures after an OS update are usually driver/firmware. A direct-monitor test isolates dock vs monitor in seconds.
hints: Direct-attach the monitors first to isolate the dock.
If multiple users on the same dock model, escalate to Desktop Support - Tier 2.
learning_objectives: Use isolation tests to prove which component is at fault
Document driver and firmware versions in work notes

tool_clues.cmdb: Asset LAP-1234 paired with DOCK-WD19 firmware 1.0.21 (latest 1.0.34).
tool_clues.endpoint: Display driver updated 6 hours ago by Windows Update.
